title: The Fall Ground

features.name: fallen instructor
features.desc-on-touch: the log where he taught you to fall, its surface worn smooth by the weight of bodies learning gravity as a teacher, not an enemy

features.name: earth worn soft
features.desc-on-touch: the spot where he landed a thousand times, the soil packed into a slight bowl, darker than surrounding ground where the archive learned what impact teaches

features.name: young eucalypt
features.desc-on-touch: a sapling growing from the fall-ground, thin and reaching, as if the earth is teaching the tree what it learned from teaching him

features.name: rope marker
features.desc-on-touch: frayed rope tied between posts, once marking the falling circle; now it sags with age, suspended between what was taught and what was learned

features.name: teaching stone
features.desc-on-touch: a dark stone placed to mark the center, half-buried now, the archive learning what the earth learned first

inscription: the earth received him and the earth began to learn what receiving meant

file.name: Training_Log_Calloway_1987-1992.txt
file.note: handwritten notes on falling technique, progressions, the year he learned to surrender, transcribed

exits.label: back into the scrub where the training started
toward the river where he learned to float after learning to fall
exits.to: NEW: the place where falling becomes water

exits.label: deeper into the scrub where students still come
exits.to: NEW: the current lineage, the next generation still learning to fall
